name: npm-jar-e2e-consumer
description: Layer 2 e2e consumer (happy path). Depends on every fixture under test/fixtures/ via relative file: specifiers so `npm install` runs each fixture's lifecycle script inside the npm-jar VM. The Layer 2 workflow runs `mode=update`, asserts the produced .npm-jar.lock.yml contains every expected attack-shape marker, then runs `mode=check` against that just-produced file and asserts exit 0. No committed golden — src/action/diff.ts is byte-exact and the lockfile's generated_at field varies per run, so a static golden cannot stay in sync.
